text:
We awarded him the gold medal for being the coolest man on the planet, andYusuf Dikeçdid not flinch. From Turkey, no hilarious glasses, no headphones, the Turkish shooter casually won theOlympicsilver medal in the 10-metre air pistol mixed team event with the air of someone who – in relaxation mode – has just had a cup of tea and is ready to go for a walk.

Yusuf Dikeç duly went viral, with countless memes circling the web for days and new fan pages dedicated to him. His name has apparently been searched over 200,000 times on Google in the United States alone.

Yusuf Dikec (Photo by Alain JOCARD / AFP) (Photo by ALAIN JOCARD/AFP via Getty Images)

The hand in the pocket pose is what stole the show – perhaps because it pointed to his totally relaxed state of mind, which is rather unusual when participating in the most important sporting event on earth. In a plain white Adidas T-shirt with Turkey across it, his attire was a far cry from the very stylishKim Yejiwho was playing a whole different game. Yet in that very minimal, basic, plain, normal core style of his, one detail has been tantalising us for more than a week. What watch is Dikeç wearing? We zoomed in as much as we could, we researched and asked experienced watch editors, but couldn't figure it out... until finallyHoroguidesidentified the brand and model of our favourite shooter's watch.

It's a military green Nacardive watch. Most likely a Nacar 07-290699-BNS6, which is a 46mm quartz diver's watch with a water resistance of 200 metres. The last known price was ₺1,386 (Turkish lira), which is equivalent to about £32. And from what we can gather, it appears to have been Dikeç's go-to watch for some time.

There is a snapshot from 2022 in which he is wearing it, but also another from 2023. So we got even more curious, and found that theNacar brandwas created specifically for the Turkish market, where it was introduced in 1942, and seems to be a common heirloom in Turkish families. There are some detailsherethat tell how the Nacar brand was the brainchild of the Turkish Armenian brothers Nacaroglu, Ohannes and Kevork. They founded the Zila Watch company in 1921 and created the Nacar brand (from the surname Nacaroglu), producing their first watch in the same year. Ohannes, who resided in Biel, Switzerland, and Kevork in Istanbul, chose Turkey, Lebanon and Syria as their target markets.

The Nacar watch entered Turkey in 1929 and has been popular ever since. So much so that apparently there was a saying that translated means ‘If you are buying a watch, it should be a Nacar; if you are getting married, it should be with a Hungarian.’ As if to say that the Nacar brand stands for high standards, and for guarantee.

Nacar 07-290699-BNS6

Attempting to get hold of this watch though seems impossible, so here are some possible alternatives that echo the shape, the colours, and the spirit of Yusuf Dikeç's favourite watch: reaching the coolness levels of the most icy man of the Paris 2024 Olympics remains a near-impossible feat, but with these timepieces on our wrists we can at least try.

title: Olympic sensation Yusuf Dikeç's watch is super rare – but costs less than £40 | British GQ
author: Paola Montanaro
description: When the Turkish shooter went viral at Paris 2024, he was wearing a mystery watch – which we're pleased to say has been identified
